The HTML class¶

class services.html.HTML(object)¶

A very lightweight class to help with HTML page rendering. It relies on an environment variable called SEMPERPY_WEB which is expected to have two subdirectories:

  • css containing cascading style sheet files (css)
  • templates containing .tmpl files as described further

Some sample files are part of the distribution in the web directory.

Public methods¶

list(items):
Parameters:items – list of elements to render in an HTML list

Returns an HMTL string using <ul> and <li> tags. List elements are the elements of the list items. The elements are expected to be strings or lists, if these are lists, this method works recursively.

classmethod services.html.webPath()¶

Returns the contents of the SEMPERPY_WEB environmement variable always ending with ‘/’. The server install a static page handler in that location, so any file (script, css, html etc...) will be served from there.

classmethod services.html.substituteVariables(template_name, **kargs)¶
Parameters:template_name (string) – name of the template to be used (SEMPERPY_WEB/templates/name.tmpl)

Substitutes keywords in the template files which are part of **kargs and part of the contents of the templare with their value, if templates/body.tmpl contains:

<!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01 Transitional//EN">
<html>
<head>
<title>__title__</title>
<link rel="StyleSheet" href="css/style.css" type="text/css" />
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=UTF-8"/>
</head>

<body>
<h1>__welcome__</h1>
__content__
</body>

The code:

from semperpy.services.hmtl import HMTL

print HTML.substituteVariables('body',welcome="Welcome to HTML",content="This is an example",title="example")

produces:

classmethod services.html.keywords(text)¶
Parameters:text (string) – any text, more likeky the contents of a template file.

returns the list of keywords used in the strings (keywords are prefixed and suffixed with ‘__’):

from semperpy.services.html import HTML

print HTML.keywords("The __weather__ is nice __today__")
['weather','nice']

Private methods¶

classmethod services.html.template(name)¶
Parameters:name (string) – the name of the template file (without .tmpl)

Reads and returns the contents of a template file.
